strand, leaving 4-nt 5' overhang.\n\nPrimer structure: 5'-[clamp >= 1nt]-GGTCTC-[1nt spacer]-[4nt overhang]-[annealing]-3'\n\"\"\"\n\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Tuple\n\ndef parse_fasta(filename: str) -> Dict[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"Parse a FASTA file into a dict of name -> sequence.\"\"\"\n    seqs = {}\n    name = None\n    seq_parts = []\n    with open(filename) as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            if line.startswith('>'):\n                if name is not None:\n                    seqs[name] = ''.join(seq_parts).upper()\n                name = line[1:].strip()\n                seq_parts = []\n            else:\n                seq_parts.append(line)\n    if name is not None:\n        seqs[name] = ''.join(seq_parts).upper()\n    return seqs\n\ndef rc(seq: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Reverse complement of a DNA sequence.\"\"\"\n    comp = {'A': 'T', 'T': 'A', 'G': 'C', 'C': 'G',\n            'a': 't', 't': 'a', 'g': 'c', 'c': 'g'}\n    return ''.join(comp[b] for b in reversed(seq))\n\ndef calc_tm(seq: str) -> float:\n    \"\"\"Calculate Tm using oligotm with specified parameters.\"\"\"\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        ['oligotm', '-tp', '1', '-sc', '1', '-mv', '50', '-dv', '2', '-n', '0.8', '-d', '500', seq],\n        capture_output=True, text=True\n    )\n    return float(result.stdout.strip())\n\ndef is_palindrome(seq: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a 4-nt sequence is a palindrome (same as its RC).\"\"\"\n    return seq == rc(seq)\n\n# Parse sequences\nseqs = parse_fasta('/app/sequences.fasta')\ninput_seq = seqs['input']\negfp_seq = seqs['egfp']\nflag_seq = seqs['flag']\nsnap_seq = seqs['snap']\noutput_seq = seqs['output']\n\nprint(f\"Input length: {len(input_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"EGFP length: {len(egfp_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"FLAG length: {len(flag_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"SNAP length: {len(snap_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"Output length: {len(output_seq)}\")\n\n# Find the junctions in the output sequence\n# The output is: backbone_part + egfp_modified + flag_modified + snap_modified + backbone_part\n# Where:\n#   - egfp_modified = egfp without stop codon (TAA)\n#   - flag_modified = flag without start (ATG) and stop (TAA)\n#   - snap_modified = snap without start (ATG)\n\n# First, find where egfp appears in the output\negfp_nostop = egfp_seq[:-3]  # Remove TAA stop codon\nflag_nostart_nostop = flag_seq[3:-3]  # Remove ATG and TAA\nsnap_nostart = snap_seq[3:]  # Remove ATG\n\nprint(f\"\\nEGFP (no stop): {egfp_nostop[:20]}...{egfp_nostop[-20:]}\")\nprint(f\"FLAG (no ATG, no TAA): {flag_nostart_nostop[:20]}...{flag_nostart_nostop[-20:]}\")\nprint(f\"SNAP (no ATG): {snap_nostart[:20]}...{snap_nostart[-20:]}\")\n\n# Verify these appear in the output\npos_egfp = output_seq.find(egfp_nostop)\nprint(f\"\\nEGFP (no stop) found at position: {pos_egfp}\")\n\npos_flag = output_seq.find(flag_nostart_nostop)\nprint(f\"FLAG (no ATG, no TAA) found at position: {pos_flag}\")\n\npos_snap = output_seq.find(snap_nostart)\nprint(f\"SNAP (no ATG) found at position: {pos_snap}\")\n\n# Verify the fusion is correct\n# egfp(no stop) + flag(no atg, no stop) + snap(no atg)\nfusion = egfp_nostop + flag_nostart_nostop + snap_nostart\npos_fusion = output_seq.find(fusion)\nprint(f\"\\nFull fusion found at position: {pos_fusion}\")\n\n# The backbone region in the output\n# Everything before the fusion and after the fusion\nbackbone_before = output_seq[:pos_fusion]\nbackbone_after = output_seq[pos_fusion + len(fusion):]\nprint(f\"\\nBackbone before fusion: ...{backbone_before[-30:]}\")\nprint(f\"Backbone after fusion: {backbone_after[:30]}...\")\n\n# Verify backbone_before + backbone_after exists in input\nbackbone_combined = backbone_after + backbone_before  # circular!\nprint(f\"\\nBackbone combined (after+before for circular): {backbone_combined[:30]}...{backbone_combined[-30:]}\")\npos_backbone = input_seq.find(backbone_combined)\nprint(f\"Backbone found in input at position: {pos_backbone}\")\n\n# Also check: does the input contain backbone_after + ... + backbone_before?\n# In the input, the gene region is between backbone_before and backbone_after\n# Let's find where backbone_before ends and backbone_after starts in the input\npos_bb_before_in_input = input_seq.find(backbone_before[-50:])\nprint(f\"\\nBackbone_before tail in input at: {pos_bb_before_in_input}\")\npos_bb_after_in_input = input_seq.find(backbone_after[:50])\nprint(f\"Backbone_after head in input at: {pos_bb_after_in_input}\")\n\n# The gene being replaced\ngene_start_in_input = pos_bb_before_in_input + 50  # approximate\ngene_region_start = len(backbone_before)  # In input's linear representation\nprint(f\"\\nIn input, backbone_before ends at position: {gene_region_start}\")\n\n# Find gene boundaries more precisely\n# backbone_before is the part of output before the fusion\n# This same sequence should appear in the input\nbb_before_in_input = input_seq.find(backbone_before)\nif bb_before_in_input == -1:\n    # Try shorter match\n    for l in range(len(backbone_before), 10, -1):\n        if input_seq.find(backbone_before[-l:]) >= 0:\n            print(f\"  Backbone before matches input at last {l} chars\")\n            break\n\nbb_after_in_input = input_seq.find(backbone_after)\nif bb_after_in_input == -1:\n    for l in range(len(backbone_after), 10, -1):\n        if I need to choose where in the output\n# sequence the 4-nt window sits.\n\n# For each junction, I'll try different window positions and pick non-palindromic\n# overhangs that are unique.\n\n# At each junction, the overhang window can be placed at different offsets\n# relative to the junction position. The constraint is:\n# - Bases before the overhang must be from the LEFT template\n# - Bases after the overhang must be from the RIGHT template\n\n# For J1 (backbone->egfp):\n#   Left template = backbone (input plasmid backbone)\n#   Right template = egfp\n#   The backbone template ends at: ...GAAGGAGATATACATAT (input sequence before gene)\n#   The egfp template starts at: ATGAGCAAGGGC...\n#   In the output: ...GAAGGAGATATACATAT|ATGAGCAAG...\n#   The overhang can be placed entirely in the backbone side, entirely in the egfp side,\n#   or spanning the boundary. EGFP template starts with ATGAGC...\n#   So the overhang CAN include the last few bases of the backbone template or\n#   the first few bases of the egfp template (these would be in the primer extension,\n#   not in the annealing region).\n\n# Let me define the junction context more precisely:\n# For J1: output[j1_pos-4:j1_pos+4] shows the 8 bases around the junction\nfor i, (name, pos) in enumerate([(\"J1\", j1_pos), (\"J2\", j2_pos), (\"J3\", j3_pos), (\"J4\", j4_pos)]):\n    context = output_seq[max(0,pos-8):pos+8]\n    print(f\"\\n{name} context (16 bases): {context}\")\n    print(f\"  Position in context: {'.'*min(8,pos) + '|' + '.'*8}\")\n    for offset in range(-4, 5):\n        oh_start = pos + offset\n        oh_end = oh_start + 4\n        if oh_start >= 0 and oh_end <= len(output_seq):\n            oh = output_seq[oh_start:oh_end]\n            pal = \"PALINDROME\" if is_palindrome(oh) else \"ok\"\n            print(f\"  Offset {offset:+d}: {oh} ({pal})\")\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":267,"cache_read_input_tokens":72387,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":267,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ab371fe6-fee0-440b-b987-e879b920e649","uuid":"f0716f50-30e8-43e6-8f8d-d011514aa93a"}

python3\n\"\"\"\nGolden Gate Assembly primer designer for BsaI-HF v2.\n\nBsaI recognition: GGTCTC\nCut: GGTCTC(1/5) -> cuts 1 nt after recognition on sense strand,\n     5 nt after on antisense strand, leaving 4-nt 5' overhang.\n\nPrimer structure: 5'-[clamp >= 1nt]-GGTCTC-[1nt spacer]-[4nt overhang]-[annealing]-3'\n\"\"\"\n\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Tuple\n\ndef parse_fasta(filename: str) -> Dict[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"Parse a FASTA file into a dict of name -> sequence.\"\"\"\n    seqs = {}\n    name = None\n    seq_parts = []\n    with open(filename) as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            if line.startswith('>'):\n                if name is not None:\n                    seqs[name] = ''.join(seq_parts).upper()\n                name = line[1:].strip()\n                seq_parts = []\n            else:\n                seq_parts.append(line)\n    if name is not None:\n        seqs[name] = ''.join(seq_parts).upper()\n    return seqs\n\ndef rc(seq: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Reverse complement of a DNA sequence.\"\"\"\n    comp = {'A': 'T', 'T': 'A', 'G': 'C', 'C': 'G',\n            'a': 't', 't': 'a', 'g': 'c', 'c': 'g'}\n    return ''.join(comp[b] for b in reversed(seq))\n\ndef calc_tm(seq: str) -> float:\n    \"\"\"Calculate Tm using oligotm with specified parameters.\"\"\"\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        ['oligotm', '-tp', '1', '-sc', '1', '-mv', '50', '-dv', '2', '-n', '0.8', '-d', '500', seq],\n        capture_output=True, text=True\n    )\n    return float(result.stdout.strip())\n\ndef is_palindrome(seq: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a 4-nt sequence is a palindrome (same as its RC).\"\"\"\n    return seq == rc(seq)\n\n# Parse sequences\nseqs = parse_fasta('/app/sequences.fasta')\ninput_seq = seqs['input']\negfp_seq = seqs['egfp']\nflag_seq = seqs['flag']\nsnap_seq = seqs['snap']\noutput_seq = seqs['output']\n\nprint(f\"Input length: {len(input_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"EGFP length: {len(egfp_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"FLAG length: {len(flag_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"SNAP length: {len(snap_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"Output length: {len(output_seq)}\")\n\n# Find the junctions in the output sequence\n# The output is: backbone_part + egfp_modified + flag_modified + snap_modified + backbone_part\n# Where:\n#   - egfp_modified = egfp without stop codon (TAA)\n#   - flag_modified = flag without start (ATG) and stop (TAA)\n#   - snap_modified = snap without start (ATG)\n\n# First, find where egfp appears in the output\negfp_nostop = egfp_seq[:-3]  # Remove TAA stop codon\nflag_nostart_nostop = flag_seq[3:-3]  # Remove ATG and TAA\nsnap_nostart = snap_seq[3:]  # Remove ATG\n\nprint(f\"\\nEGFP (no stop): {egfp_nostop[:20]}...{egfp_nostop[-20:]}\")\nprint(f\"FLAG (no ATG, no TAA): {flag_nostart_nostop[:20]}...{flag_nostart_nostop[-20:]}\")\nprint(f\"SNAP (no ATG): {snap_nostart[:20]}...{snap_nostart[-20:]}\")\n\n# Verify these appear in the output\npos_egfp = output_seq.find(egfp_nostop)\nprint(f\"\\nEGFP (no stop) found at position: {pos_egfp}\")\n\npos_flag = output_seq.find(flag_nostart_nostop)\nprint(f\"FLAG (no ATG, no TAA) found at position: {pos_flag}\")\n\npos_snap = output_seq.find(snap_nostart)\nprint(f\"SNAP (no ATG) found at position: {pos_snap}\")\n\n# Verify the fusion is correct\n# egfp(no stop) + flag(no atg, no stop) + snap(no atg)\nfusion = egfp_nostop + flag_nostart_nostop + snap_nostart\npos_fusion = output_seq.find(fusion)\nprint(f\"\\nFull fusion found at position: {pos_fusion}\")\n\n# The backbone region in the output\n# Everything before the fusion and after the fusion\nbackbone_before = output_seq[:pos_fusion]\nbackbone_after = output_seq[pos_fusion + len(fusion):]\nprint(f\"\\nBackbone before fusion: ...{backbone_before[-30:]}\")\nprint(f\"Backbone after fusion: {backbone_after[:30]}...\")\n\n# Verify backbone_before + backbone_after exists in input\nbackbone_combined = backbone_after + backbone_before  # circular!\nprint(f\"\\nBackbone combined (after+before
